decision_point: In an adult with early or mild thumb carpometacarpal (basal joint) osteoarthritis, is splinting plus injection or trapeziectomy the preferred first-line?
clinical_vignette:
Typical adult patient for whom this decision arises (no atypical comorbidities or contraindications).

Decision: In an adult with early or mild thumb carpometacarpal (basal joint) osteoarthritis, is splinting plus injection or trapeziectomy the preferred first-line?
Options:
  1. Splinting plus corticosteroid injection
  2. Trapeziectomy
label: settled
label_rationale: Non-operative care is first-line for early disease; surgery is reserved for advanced or refractory arthritis.
